personal_profile: A dedicated Mechatronics graduate with hands-on experience in test rig development and PLC programming. Highly capable of bridging the gap between mechanical hardware and data analysis to solve complex engineering problems. Brings a strong track record of building and troubleshooting automated test setups across the aerospace and industrial power sectors. Passionate about machine reliability and safety, and looking to apply these technical skills to ensure product durability within the Test & Validation team at Caterpillar Desford.

bullets: The degree offers a highly practical grounding in robotics, industrial automation, and embedded systems.

A strong emphasis is placed on engineering simulation and control systems, providing the exact theoretical framework required to accurately assess machine capabilities, monitor performance results, and ensure long-term reliability in physical components.

Dissertation: Investigated the application of PID control algorithms and sensor fusion to improve system efficiency. This project involved extensive data monitoring and required the practical tuning of control setups, directly mirroring the skills needed for instrumentation and testing procedures.

education.degree: Diploma in Mechatronics & Smart Factory (NOCN Level 5)
bullets: Provided a comprehensive, hands-on grounding in PLC programming, industrial robotics, and applied mechanics.
The programme culminated in an academic project developing an 'Intelligent Vehicle Speed Control System'.

This involved designing a closed-loop control system utilizing STM32 microcontrollers and Hall Effect sensors to implement real-time adaptive algorithms, securing 2nd Prize in a national technical evaluation.

professional_qualifications.skills: PLC Programming (Siemens S7-1200, Allen Bradley), Test Rig Development, Calibration Verification, LabVIEW (Familiarity).
Python (Pandas/Scripting), Power BI (DAX), SQL, Microsoft Excel (Power Query), KPI Monitoring.
Certified Machinery Safety Expert (ISO 13849 & IEC 62061), High Voltage (HV) Awareness, NDT Level II (VT, PT, MT, UT, RT).

work_experience.company: BAE Systems (via UCLan EASE), Preston, UK
work_experience.context: Tasked with ensuring the reliability of safety-critical aerospace components, focusing on building test setups and automating data collection to improve validation efficiency.
achievements: Programmed a Safety PLC and SCADA interface for an aerospace test rig, designing a custom auto-diagnostic routine that reduced troubleshooting time by 30%.
Developed Python scripts to automate the extraction of raw CSV test logs into live Power BI dashboards, creating a reliable tool for the team to monitor performance metrics in real time.
Conducted calibration verification and mechanical torque audits during Factory Acceptance Testing (FAT), achieving a 100% first-time pass rate while upholding strict safety standards.
Redesigned critical actuator brackets in SolidWorks and validated their structural integrity through FEA simulations, reducing mass by 12% without compromising the safety factor.

work_experience.context: Specialized in the hands-on troubleshooting, integration, and maintenance of heavy industrial power generation and utility systems across multiple client sites.
achievements: Investigated and resolved faults on legacy diesel generators by retrofitting Deep Sea Electronics (DSE) controllers and updating PLC logic, restoring critical backup power and cutting start-up failures by 40%.
Audited pneumatic distribution lines to identify process inefficiencies, successfully pitching and installing Variable Speed Drive (VSD) compressors to lower energy costs by 15%.
Carried out routine calibration verification and breakdown maintenance on industrial power units, ensuring high asset availability and compliance with client Service Level Agreements.

work_experience.context: Focused on improving testing procedures and quality control within a fast-paced, multi-national manufacturing environment.
achievements: Designed and built an automated valve pressure-cycling test rig using a Siemens S7-1200 PLC, replacing manual testing methods and increasing testing throughput by 20%.
Investigated valve seat leakages by applying Root Cause Analysis (RCA) and Fishbone diagrams, successfully identifying machining tolerance errors and reducing rework by 25%.

bullets: Actively participate in technical workshops and peer-mentoring sessions. Routinely present complex engineering concepts to diverse audiences, fostering a collaborative learning environment and demonstrating a commitment to teamwork and shared success.
